parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes the relative/controller-centric clause (Am 98); recital 27 reworded to 'same or higher' protection, CJEU reading, independent supervisory authorities (Am 17). JURI silent (data-acquis only). Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending; Kaljurand/centre-left hostile to redefinition
eesc: Must stay objective and adequately broad; broad CJEU reading

status: Council deletes; likely consensus to drop

parliament: No committee amendment on the GDPR research definition (JURI/IMCO data-acquis only); open pending joint ITRE/LIBE report
eesc: Keep commonly-accepted meaning (EDPB/CJEU); enable industrial research

parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes Art 9(2)(k) and 9(5) (Am 100-101) and recital 33 (Am 20) — opposes the 'AI privilege' as contrary to necessity/proportionality. Stronger than Council. Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
member_states: FR/PL want incidental-only + 'disproportionate effort' clarified; RO wants SME-proportionate; NL: if kept, narrow to 'technically impossible' (drop 'disproportionate effort')

parliament: No committee amendment; likely support tightening pending joint ITRE/LIBE report
member_states: FR wants Union/MS-law gate; PL restrictive + tech-neutral reading

commission: Refuse where reasonable grounds to believe other purpose
council_st9547: Retained but requires abusive intention demonstrated
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes the abuse-of-rights restriction (Am 103-106) and adds an anti-circumvention/anti-dark-pattern duty barring abusive resort to Art 12(5) (Am 107, DMA Art 13-inspired). Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
eesc: Right of access is enabling; burden of proving abuse stays with controller
member_states: PL: burden on controller (welcomed) but read restrictively; FR: also catch procedure circumvention; NL: support 'abusive intention' standard (per CJEU/EDPB)

parliament: IMCO recital 44 tightens the carve-out to 'strictly necessary and proportionate' / 'explicitly requested' (Am 21); no operative Art 13 amendment. Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
member_states: FR supports; PL keep strictly low-risk; research derogation exceptional

council_st9547: Largely restored to current GDPR; (a) clause deleted
parliament: No committee amendment on Art 22 (IMCO/JURI); likely side with the Council de-escalation pending joint ITRE/LIBE report
eesc: ADM must respect data-minimisation; human decision preferred where reasonable
member_states: FR backs keeping current Art 22; PL: refine not delete; EE open to COM amendment; NL: do NOT amend Art 22 (backs EDPB/EDPS/AP)

council_st9547: High-risk threshold; 96h; via national entry point
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877): EDPB makes the breach template public and the Commission shall take utmost account of the Board (Am 108-109); new safeguard that the single entry point must not lower security/privacy/consumer/data-protection levels or limit supervisory powers (Am 110). Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending

member_states: PL/RO: single clear 96h (flag 72h/96h ambiguity); PL keep implementing-act for template/lists

parliament: No committee amendment on Art 35 (IMCO/JURI); likely support EDPB-led list pending joint ITRE/LIBE report
member_states: PL opposes EDPB-establishes model (wants implementing act); RO wants light SME DPIA templates

council_st9547: GDPR Art 88a DELETED; consent kept in ePrivacy Art 5(3)
parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877): limits gatekeepers to one consent prompt/year per refused purpose and voids consent re-requested within the no-repeat window as an unfair practice under Dir 2005/29 (Am 115-116); opposes deleting ePrivacy Art 4 (Am 122). Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
eesc: Explore simplified consent in low-risk contexts (risk-based)
member_states: FR/PL keep cookie rules in ePrivacy (Presidency adopted); FR also wants Art 88b deleted

parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) strengthens the signal regime: standardisation request within 3 months with balanced stakeholder representation (Am 117), extends the duty to all 'software to access online interfaces' (tech-neutral) and drops the SME exemption (Am 118), adds third-party-software + anti-dark-pattern duties (Am 119), and accelerates the browser/OS duty from 48 to 18 months (Am 120); DPA powers and fines extended to software providers (Am 112-114). Opposite to FR/industry deletion. Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
member_states: FR demands deletion (no impact assessment); EE: browsers should enable withdrawal

parliament: IMCO draft opinion (PE789.877) deletes Art 88c (Am 121) and recitals 30-31 (Am 18-19) — aligns with the Council deletion. EDRi/noyb push deletion; EPP more supportive of a recital. Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
member_states: RO: clarify/remove 'regurgitation' (rec 31); EE: don't unduly restrict legal bases; PL prior comments stand

commission: ENISA SEP; report once share many
council_st9547: Retained (national entry points + ENISA info point)

eesc: Strong support; English submissions + reuse + need-to-know access
member_states: DK/EE want MS role + later timeline review; ES direct DORA/CER/eIDAS channel; FI voluntary; AT Art 38 ambiguity

commission: Repeal and consolidate into Data Act
council_st9547: DGA/FFNPD/Open Data repealed; P2B partially amended
parliament: JURI (PE789.142, 68 amdts) and IMCO (PE789.877, 124 amdts) committee-for-opinion drafts largely reverse the deregulation: oppose the P2B repeal (JURI Am 12; IMCO Am 123-124, demanding an impact assessment), keep smart contracts/Art 36 (JURI Am 7/36), restore cloud switching (JURI Am 34), restore DGA intermediation neutrality + mandatory registration (JURI Am 13-14/37-42), restore B2G public-interest access (JURI Am 28/31); IMCO lets public bodies set higher re-use charges for gatekeepers (Am 15-16). Joint ITRE/LIBE report pending
ecb: Welcomes Data Act consolidation + FFNPD (2018/1807) repeal; supply public-interest data at cost
eesc: Limit mandatory data-sharing to strictly necessary; strengthen EDIB; favour voluntary sharing
